summary: The principle secretory cells located at the outermost margin of the pulp include odontoblasts which play a crucial role in forming dentin directly adjacent to tooth structure boundaries. Odontoblast activity is essential due to continuous interaction needed across those specific regions where they interface closely with both internal pulp elements and external dentinal layers. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related to identifying such specialized cellular entities involved primarily in direct secretion activities towards exterior-facing tissue domains pertinent herein includes recognition that it’s predominantly about understanding functional localization alongside typical duties performed thereupon.
